pull.title: BIP-0322: polishing follow-up
pull.body: Addresses a couple of review comments in https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2141 as a follow-up.

labels.name: BIP Update by Owner
labels.description: PR by Author or Deputy to modify their own BIP

events.body:
Thanks for following up @guggero!

Here are some suggested edits. Feel free to pick and choose (or for the BIP names, adopt a different format to be used consistently):

https://github.com/jonatack/bips/commits/2026-05-bip322-suggested-edits-or-areas-of-feedback/

Thanks a lot for the review and suggested fixes, @jonatack! Took almost all of them, really appreciate the inputs.

I also added a commit to clarify the use of the `SIGHASH_DEFAULT` for Taproot-based inputs.

Left some comments. 
It's my first time reading BIP322, so I also left some comments outside of the current PR diff :)
Good improvements. I just have one additional suggestion.

events.body: Sorry for the piece-meal review: I noticed an open parenthesis that isn’t being closed on this read. Other than that all the changes looks good to me. Let’s get this shipped. :)

events.message:
BIP-0322: grammar and readability touchup

Co-authored-by: Jon Atack <jon@atack.com>

events.body:
Looks good to me! I read the whole document, but I didn't do an in depth review of the "Detailed Specification" section.

Thanks for moving the Proof of Funds part outside the motivation, and congrats on becoming a BIP author :)

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -33,7 +34,8 @@ interoperability with existing signing hardware, we also define a signature mess
 resembles a Bitcoin transaction (except that it contains an invalid input, so it cannot be spent on
 any real network).
 
-The Proof of Funds variant allows demonstrating control of a set of UTXOs.
+The [[bip-0322.mediawiki#full-proof-of-funds|Proof of Funds]] variant allows demonstrating control
comments.body:
Do you think an anchor link would be best here?

See just-merged https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2091/changes for the format.

```Let ''T'' be```

Highlighting this typo separately.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,31 +22,31 @@
 == Abstract ==
 
 A standard for interoperable signed messages based on the Bitcoin Script format, either for proving
-fund availability, or committing to a message as the intended recipient of funds sent to the invoice
-address.
+availability of funds, or for committing to a message as the intended recipient of funds sent to the
+invoice address.
 
 == Motivation ==
 
 The current message signing standard only works for P2PKH (1...) invoice addresses. We propose to
-extend and generalize the standard by using a Bitcoin Script based approach. This ensures that any
-coins, no matter what script they are controlled by, can in-principle be signed for. For easy
-interoperability with existing signing hardware, we also define a signature message format which
+extend and generalize the standard by using an approach based on a Bitcoin Script. This ensures that
+any coins, no matter what script they are controlled by, can in principle be signed for. For easy
+interoperability with existing signing hardware, we also define a signature message format that
 resembles a Bitcoin transaction (except that it contains an invalid input, so it cannot be spent on
 any real network).
 
 The [[#full-proof-of-funds|Proof of Funds]] variant allows demonstrating control
 of a set of UTXOs in addition to the message being signed.
 The list of UTXOs may or may not be related to the address being signed with (the
comments.body: I'm a bit unsure about this sentence... I think this is an implementation detail that doesn't really belong in the motivation. Also, before reading the whole BIP, I found this sentence to be a bit confusing, it almost reads as if a signer could prove ownership of arbitrary UTXOs, including ones they don't control.

comments.diff_hunk: @@ -153,14 +153,14 @@ The to_spend transaction is: vout[0].nValue = 0 vout[0].scriptPubKey = message_challenge -where message_hash is a BIP340-tagged hash of the message, i.e. sha256_tag(m), where -tag = BIP0322-signed-message and m is the message as is without length +where message_hash is a BIP340-tagged hash of the message, i.e., sha256_tag(m), where +tag = BIP0322-signed-message and m is the message as-is without length prefix or null terminator, and message_challenge is the to be proven (public) key -script. +script to be proven.
comments.body: this sentence contains "to be proven" twice: " is the **to be proven** (public) key script **to be proven**."

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -455,8 +455,8 @@ A '''transaction finalizer''' of a BIP-0322 PSBT must follow these steps:
 
 == Compatibility ==
 
-This specification is backwards compatible with the legacy signmessage/verifymessage specification
-through the special case as described above.
+This specification is backwards-compatible with the legacy signmessage/verifymessage specification
+through the special case [#legacy|as described above].
comments.body: The `[#legacy|as described above]` is not a link on Github, and is shown as this: I think you want it written as: `[[#legacy|as described above]]`. Also, you may want to move this to the second commit (it's in the fourth one at the moment).
`to_spend` is not formatted correctly, it's `to_spend` but should be `to_spend` (note the extra / in the opening tag)

comments.diff_hunk: @@ -153,14 +153,14 @@ The to_spend transaction is: vout[0].nValue = 0 vout[0].scriptPubKey = message_challenge -where message_hash is a BIP340-tagged hash of the message, i.e. sha256_tag(m), where -tag = BIP0322-signed-message and m is the message as is without length +where message_hash is a BIP340-tagged hash of the message, i.e., sha256_tag(m), where +tag = BIP0322-signed-message and m is the message as-is without length prefix or null terminator, and message_challenge is the to be proven (public) key -script. +script to be proven.
comments.body: suggest dropping the first occurrence and keeping the second one

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -1076,7 +1076,7 @@ users (see also: [https://en.bitcoin.it/wiki/Economic_majority economic majority
 | [[bip-0322.mediawiki|322]]
 | Applications
 | Generic Signed Message Format
-| Karl-Johan Alm
+| Karl-Johan Alm, guggero
comments.body: In the README, author names tend to be capitalized, and mostly real names instead of GitHub pseudonyms. It might be more a convention than a rule, though.

comments.diff_hunk: @@ -16,35 +16,37 @@ 2025-05-10: https://bitcoin.stackexchange.com/questions/126277/where-can-i-use-bip322-to-sign-a-message-to-verify-a-multisig-address 2026-04-20: https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoindev/c/qd6BNz9gxCk/m/k1fHq4RKAQAJ Version: 1.0.0 + Requires: 174, 340, 341 == Abstract == A standard for interoperable signed messages based on the Bitcoin Script format, either for proving -fund availability, or committing to a message as the intended recipient of funds sent to the invoice -address. +availability of funds, or for committing to a message as the intended recipient of funds sent to the +invoice address. == Motivation == The current message signing standard only works for P2PKH (1...) invoice addresses. We propose to -extend and generalize the standard by using a Bitcoin Script based approach. This ensures that any -coins, no matter what script they are controlled by, can in-principle be signed for. For easy -interoperability with existing signing hardware, we also define a signature message format which +extend and generalize the standard by using an approach based on a Bitcoin Script. This ensures that
comments.body:
Not sure, but I think you wanted to write "an approach based on Bitcoin Script" here, rather than "an approach based on a Bitcoin Script" (and the first one seems closer to the current meaning).
```suggestion
extend and generalize the standard by using an approach based on Bitcoin Script. This ensures that
```

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -1076,7 +1076,7 @@ users (see also: [https://en.bitcoin.it/wiki/Economic_majority economic majority
 | [[bip-0322.mediawiki|322]]
 | Applications
 | Generic Signed Message Format
-| Karl-Johan Alm
+| Karl-Johan Alm, guggero
comments.body: The value in the README has to match the value in the Authors header in the document, so if you capitalize one, capitalize both.

comments.diff_hunk: @@ -247,7 +249,7 @@ Validation consists of the following steps: ## Confirm that the two transactions together satisfy all consensus rules, except for to_spend's missing input, and except that ''nSequence'' of to_sign's first input and ''nLockTime'' of to_sign are not checked. # (Optional) If the validator does not have a full script interpreter, it should check that it understands all scripts being satisfied. If not, it should stop here and output ''inconclusive''. # Check the '''required rules''': -## All signatures must use the SIGHASH_ALL flag. +## All signatures must use the SIGHASH_ALL (or SIGHASH_DEFAULT for [[bip-0341.mediawiki|BIP341 P2TR]] inputs) flag.
comments.body: The phrasing here feels a bit cumbersome. How about: ```suggestion ## All signatures MUST use the SIGHASH_ALL flag, unless the output type supports SIGHASH_DEFAULT, which then MAY be used alternatively (e.g., [[bip-0341.mediawiki|BIP341 P2TR]]). ```

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,31 +22,31 @@
 == Abstract ==
 
 A standard for interoperable signed messages based on the Bitcoin Script format, either for proving
-fund availability, or committing to a message as the intended recipient of funds sent to the invoice
-address.
+availability of funds, or for committing to a message as the intended recipient of funds sent to the
+invoice address.
 
 == Motivation ==
 
 The current message signing standard only works for P2PKH (1...) invoice addresses. We propose to
-extend and generalize the standard by using a Bitcoin Script based approach. This ensures that any
-coins, no matter what script they are controlled by, can in-principle be signed for. For easy
-interoperability with existing signing hardware, we also define a signature message format which
+extend and generalize the standard by using an approach based on a Bitcoin Script. This ensures that
+any coins, no matter what script they are controlled by, can in principle be signed for. For easy
+interoperability with existing signing hardware, we also define a signature message format that
 resembles a Bitcoin transaction (except that it contains an invalid input, so it cannot be spent on
 any real network).
 
 The [[#full-proof-of-funds|Proof of Funds]] variant allows demonstrating control
 of a set of UTXOs in addition to the message being signed.
 The list of UTXOs may or may not be related to the address being signed with (the
comments.body: You're right, this sounds weird in the motivation. I moved the section to the definition of the Proof of Funds variant and also attempted to make it more clear in what it means. What do you think?

comments.body: Ah yes, that didn't make sense at all. Fixed.

If not, it should stop here and output ''inconclusive''. # Check the '''required rules''': -## All signatures must use the SIGHASH_ALL flag. +## All signatures must use the SIGHASH_ALL (or SIGHASH_DEFAULT for [[bip-0341.mediawiki|BIP341 P2TR]] inputs) flag.

For example, it may consist of outputs paid to that address,
comments.body: There is at least a closing parenthesis missing here, but the first sentence reads a bit wonky.

For example, it may consist of outputs paid to that address,
